prs.title: docs: add transformers section to markdown-it
prs.body:
docs: add transformers section to markdown-it

Adds a paragraph and example on how to use transformers with the `@shikijs/markdown-it` plugin.

Closes #722

prs.title: feat: change Webview::navigate to accept WebviewUrl instead of Url

labels.name: type: breaking change
labels.description: This issue or pull request will introduce a breaking change and requires major version bump

prs.body:
This commit addresses issue #14583 by changing the  and
 methods to accept  as a parameter
instead of the raw  type.

Changes:
- Updated  to accept  parameter
- Updated  to accept  parameter
- Added  helper method that converts  to using the same logic as webview initialization (handles dev server proxying, app URLs, external URLs, and custom protocols)
- Added comprehensive documentation examples for both methods

Benefits:
- Type-safety: Ensures URLs passed to navigate use the same type as webview creation
- Consistency: Uses the same URL handling logic as webview initialization
- Fixes dev server proxy issues: Properly handles localhost/dev server URLs
- Better API: More intuitive for developers using WebviewUrl elsewhere

Breaking Change:
This is a breaking change as the method signature has changed from:
  pub fn navigate(&self, url: Url) -> crate::Result<()>
to:
  pub fn navigate(&self, webview_url: WebviewUrl) -> crate::Result<()>

Users will need to update their code to use WebviewUrl instead of raw Url.

Migration example:
Before: webview.navigate("http://tauri.localhost/".parse()?) After:  webview.navigate(WebviewUrl::App("index.html".into()))

Closes #14583

<!--
Before submitting a PR, please read https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-request-guidelines

1. Give the PR a descriptive title.

  Examples of good title:
    - fix(windows): fix race condition in event loop
    - docs: update example for `App::show`
    - feat: add `Window::set_fullscreen`

  Examples of bad title:
    - fix #7123
    - update docs
    - fix bugs

2. If there is a related issue, reference it in the PR text, e.g. closes #123.
3. If this change requires a new version, then add a change file in `.changes` directory with the appropriate bump, see https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/.changes/README.md
4. Ensure that all your commits are signed https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/managing-commit-signature-verification/signing-commits
5. Ensure `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` passes.
6. Propose your changes as a draft PR if your work is still in progress.
-->

prs.title: fix: allow middleware to override error responses with redirects
prs.body:
This change enables middleware to handle errors gracefully by allowing them to return redirect responses even after the error handler has been invoked. Previously, when an error occurred in a downstream handler, the error handler's response would be set and finalized, preventing middleware from providing alternative responses like redirects.

The fix adds an additional condition in the compose function to check if there's an error and the returned response is different from the current context response. This allows middleware to override error responses while maintaining backward compatibility.

Fixes #4530

### The author should do the following, if applicable

- [ ] Add tests
- [ ] Run tests
- [ ] `bun run format:fix && bun run lint:fix` to format the code
- [ ] Add [TSDoc](https://tsdoc.org/)/[JSDoc](https://jsdoc.app/about-getting-started) to document the code

prs.title: fix: make Date data test locale-independent
prs.body:
The "Date data" test was failing in non-English environments due to locale-specific date formatting. The test was checking for exact string matches of Date.toString() output, which varies by locale and timezone (e.g., "01/01/2020" in US vs "01.01.2020" in Germany).

Changes:
- Updated test to use regex patterns instead of exact string matching
- Verify dates are rendered correctly without depending on locale
- Still verify tick positions remain accurate
- Test now passes in any locale/timezone environment

Fixes #6689

prs.title: fix: prevent janky login screen flash after successful authentication
prs.body:
Fix: Prevent janky login screen flash after successful authentication

Problem

After a user successfully logs in, the login screen briefly flashes for a split second before redirecting to the home page. This creates a janky user experience.

Steps to reproduce

  1. Go to the login screen

  2. Enter username and password

  3. Click “Sign In”

  4. Loading screen appears

  5. Login screen flashes briefly ← Bug

  6. User is redirected to the portal

Solution

A new local isAuthenticating state was added to the Login component. This state:

  • Is set to true immediately when authentication succeeds (before afterLogin() is called)

  • Prevents the login form from rendering by returning null when authentication is in progress

  • Provides a clean, flash-free transition from the login screen to the authenticated home page

Changes Made

File: index.tsx

  1. Added isAuthenticating state variable (line 51)

  2. Set isAuthenticating to true in callback authentication flow
    (lines 130–132)

  3. Set isAuthenticating to true in password authentication flow
    (lines 142–151)

  4. Added early return to prevent rendering during authentication
    (lines 201–204)

Testing

  • All 25 existing Login component tests pass

  • No TypeScript compilation errors

  • No regressions introduced

  • Tested both authentication flows:

    • Password authentication (email/password)

    • Callback authentication (SSO/OAuth redirect)

Test command

npm run test

Impact

Before

User clicks “Sign In” → Loading → afterLogin() → Event dispatched → State updated → Redirect initiated → Login screen flashes → Redirected to home

After

User clicks “Sign In” → Loading → isAuthenticating = true → Login form hidden immediately → afterLogin() → Redirect → Smooth transition with no flash

Visual Comparison

Before | After -- | -- Login → Flash → Home | Login → Home

Related Issue

Fixes the issue:
“Loading screen appears and for a split second login screen is shown.”

Checklist

  • Code follows project style guidelines

  • Self-review performed

  • Code commented where necessary

  • No new warnings generated

  • Existing tests pass

  • Works for both authentication flows (password & callback)

  • No breaking changes introduced

For Reviewers

Key areas to review

  1. isAuthenticating state management logic

  2. Early return condition (lines 201–204)

  3. Both authentication flows:

    • Password flow (around line 147)

    • Callback flow (around line 131)

Testing instructions

  1. Test password-based login

  2. Test callback-based login (if SSO is configured)

  3. Verify no visual flash occurs

  4. Ensure error handling behaves correctly

--- > [!NOTE] > Add isAuthenticating state to suppress rendering of the Login form during auth (callback and password flows), preventing a brief flash before redirect. > > - **Login (`frontend/src/container/Login/index.tsx`)** > - Introduce `isAuthenticating` state and update component return type to `JSX.Element | null`. > - Set `isAuthenticating` to `true` when: > - Callback flow returns with `accessToken` and `refreshToken`. > - Password flow succeeds before calling `afterLogin`. > - Early-return `null` when `isAuthenticating` to avoid rendering the login form during transition. > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit cdcdf104bb8246db75377e2b9af1f728605ac8ce. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).
